key_insight: Interface symmetry and ferroelectric barrier control, not CrSb bulk spin splitting alone, enable giant multistate resistance switching in altermagnetic multiferroic tunnel junctions.
engineering_relevance: The work suggests a design path for stray-field-free, high-ratio spintronic memory and logic devices by tuning terminations, insertion layers, and ferroelectric polarization.
method: Density functional theory to model CrSb/α-In2Se3 junction electronic structure
Nonequilibrium Green’s function calculations to evaluate spin-dependent transport and bias response
Interface engineering with Cr/Sb terminations plus h-BN or graphene insertion layers
evidence_signal: The abstract reports four-state and eight-state nonvolatile resistance behavior, with TMR/TER reaching up to 3.77×10^4% and 4.18×10^5% in bilayer In2Se3 junctions.
watch_item: The predicted performance depends on precise interface termination, layer alignment, and Fermi-level conditions, which may be challenging to realize and control experimentally.
paper_title: Interface-Engineered Giant Multistate Resistance Switching in Altermagnetic CrSb Multiferroic Tunnel Junctions
